content: Self-Refine’s task-specific iterative output polishing can be paired with Reflexion’s long-form dynamic memory to create a tiered self-improvement system for autonomous agents, where small, iterative self-feedback on immediate outputs is anchored to cumulative reflected memory of past failures and patterns. This framework would retain Self-Refine’s advantage of no extra training or supervised data while fixing its limitation of getting stuck in local minima by drawing on broader reflected context stored in Reflexion’s memory.
